hypothesis: Enhancing steric complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/ECL3/TM5/TM6 residues through aromatic substitutions at position 5 and hydrophobic substitutions at position 11 will increase selectivity margin by improving SSTR2 binding while reducing off-target affinity.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: To achieve SSTR2 selectivity, it is necessary to specifically disrupt conserved pocket interactions at non-pharmacophore positions 1/2/5/6/11/12. Targeted mutations at these positions will improve Δmargin without compromising SSTR2 binding.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are known to contribute to off-target binding (SSTR3/SSTR5) while not being part of the pharmacophore. Focusing on these positions allows for targeted mutations to disrupt off-target interactions.
These mutations are designed to disrupt conserved pocket interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 while maintaining compatibility with the SSTR2 pharmacophore and preserving cysteine residues (Cys3/Cys14).
